entry.title: Documentation of the XML structure of the Calculation view
entry.content:
Hello, is there a reasonably documented XML definition of the Calculation Views for SAP HANA? Like what are possible nodes, attributes, inner references, etc.

Thanks in advance!

Is there an update on this topic? Will this fields available in the future or do we need to request this as a feature?

BR Kerem

entry.title: how to delete Bex query (meta info screwed up...)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I wanted copy a query elements to unmatched structure ( some infoobjects are not in  )

I find the blog (https://medium.com/@mony.maheshsingh/force-copy-queries-from-one-info-provider-to-another-with-different-structure-fb615451d48f)

and followed the instruction ( To make Force Copy Program ).

And... the side effects occurred...

Two same (compuid info is also same) query object are absent in two different Info Cube...

I wanted to copy it with changing the quey and it's restricted keyfigure's name... 

As the two objects meta info are same... , i cannot delete it (last one)..

 

help me...

entry.title: Re: Error FAA_POST214 when reversing Asset Document of Le...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Have you checked the note below already ?

2544536 - [S4HANA] How to avoid error FAA_POST214 and AA370 in legacy data transfer processing if 2 fiscal years are open.

It might be helpful to solve your issue.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Exchange Rate Difference Line with Zero Amount Prevents Posting (Public Cloud)
entry.content:
When making a payment to a vendor using the same currency (TWD), SAP generates a Gain from exchange rate difference posting to G/L account 72510000, but both the debit and credit amounts are shown as zero.

In this case, the vendor invoice is linked to a down payment.

 

Because of this, I assume the posting to G/L account 72510000 should not occur and may not be necessary at all.

However, due to this G/L line item, I’m unable to complete the posting.

Has anyone else encountered the same issue or found a solution?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: ICMR How can we manage the CLEARING STATUS = 3 in the...
entry.content: Please check the sap note: 3326660-New Function for Date Fields in Display Groups of Reconciliation Case Example use cases: •To include only the items remain open before the end of a fiscal period, you set the following filters in the display group: ◦Clearing Date Is Empty (use the date function IS_EMPTY) ◦Clearing Date is After the input period (use the date function LATER_THAN_LAST_DAY_OF_PERIOD) Note: The two filters have the "OR" relationship becasue they use the same filter field. Later when viewing Reconciliation Balances, you select the display group and input a period, e.g. 008/2022, only the items who are still open on 008/2022 or have a clearing date later than the last day of 008/2022 are displayed. •To include only items remain cleared before the end of a fiscal period, you set the following filters in the display group: ◦Clearing Date is Before or on the input period (use the date function UP_TO_LAST_DAY_OF_PERIOD) ◦Clearing Status equal to 3 (Cleared) Note: The two filters have the "AND" relationship. Later when viewing Reconciliation Balances, you select the display group and input a period, e.g. 008/2022, only the items who are cleared on 008/2022 and have a clearing date on or earlier than the last day of 008/2022 are displayed.

entry.title: Inquiry about setting the default values for depreciation execution options
entry.content:
Dear. SAP Support

I hope this message finds you well

Below Image

Can I set the following option to be checked by default?

If users miss selecting this option, journal entries may be generated without the cost center for depreciation expenses.

Best Regards,

Daesung Park

entry.title: DM and STC integration - creation of a datasource in Office
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

I was trying to create a datasource in SAP Disclosure Management (stack 2202) to connect it to SAP Sustainability Control Tower. In this stack datasource connection seems to be available:

 

However the following error message is showed, after choosing a new datasource with STC system.

Do I need to implement something on the server before to create a new datasource in the client?

Regards,

Andrea Migliore

entry.title: Re: ASR Integration With SAP EWM - Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for this information @Muhammad_Ashraf .

We are trying to implement ASR (TM and EWM embedded in the same box).

It works fine for normal outbound scenario. However, when we try to integrate with GTS system (separate box), the delivery either remains blocked, or we are unable to create warehouse task with an error message shown as per the screenshot.

Would it be possible for you to point us if any additional configuration required when GTS compliance check in place for outbound FU and FO?

Thanks

entry.title: Can MTA integrated with HashiCorp Vault?
entry.content:
Hi team,

while deploying MTA package to BTP, I want to get secrets from HashiCorp Vault, do you know if MTA can be integrated with Vault? if not, do we have other secrets management systems can be integrated with Vault?

thanks 

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model 

Cherry

Is this helpful to you or do you have further questions about it? Thank you.

Kind regards,
Taleh

entry.title: Exploring New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 and QRC 01/2025
entry.content:
Related Blogs:
  • Unlocking the True Potential of Data in Files with SAP HANA Database SQL on Files in SAP HANA Cloud

As I mentioned in my blog last year, the SQL on Files capability in SAP HANA Cloud has unlocked new potential by providing direct read-only SQL access to files stored in SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files since SAP HANA Cloud QRC 03/2024.

Starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024, this capability has been further extended to support direct access to the most common external object storages listed below, in addition to SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files.

  • Amazon S3
  • Azure Blob Storage or Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS) Gen2
  • Google Cloud Storage (GCS)

Although the method for creating a remote source varies by cloud service provider, the process for creating virtual tables, the supported file formats, and the read-only access remain the same as when using SQL on Files with SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files. Since these aspects are already explained in my previous blog, let me focus on the differences in this one.

Additionally, as you may have already noticed from the above diagram, read-only access to external Delta Sharing is also supported from SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 with a dedicated Delta Sharing adapter.

And when it comes to CSV formats, two new options are now included to provide better flexibility and reduce the effort required when reading files in CSV format, starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024.

Another piece of good news is that the existing limitation on Delta tables, which currently supports only reader version 1 for both direct file access and Delta sharing, has been lifted starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 01/2025.

Alright, with these points in mind, let's dive deeper into the details with some examples.

 

Accessing external object storages

As highlighted, starting from SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024, the SAP HANA Database SQL on Files feature has begun to support direct read-only access to files (CSV, Parquet, and Delta tables) stored in external object storage.

The supported external object storage options are, as mentioned above, Amazon S3, Azure Blob Storage or Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS) Gen2, and Google Cloud Storage (GCS).

For Amazon S3 and GCS, both path-style and virtual-host-style endpoints are supported (GCS also supports path-style regional endpoints). For Azure, both standard and DNS endpoints are supported.

The methods for creating a remote source differ according to the specific standards and integrations established by each cloud service provider. The example below shows how to create a remote source to Amazon S3.

-- create a remote source to Amazon S3
CREATE REMOTE SOURCE <remote_source_name> ADAPTER "file" CONFIGURATION '
provider=s3;
endpoint=<s3_endpoint>;'
WITH CREDENTIAL TYPE 'PASSWORD' USING 'user=<access_key>;password=<secret_key>;

As described, the file adapter is still in use but now with the parameter provider=s3.

A remote source can also be created via the SAP HANA Database Explorer UI, and the example below shows how to create a remote source to Google Cloud Storage via the UI.

Please refer to the links below for further details on creating a remote source for each external object storage.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create an Amazon S3 Remote Source
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create a Microsoft Azure Storage Remote Source
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create a Google Cloud Storage Remote Source

Although the process for creating a remote source differs, as mentioned above, the method to create a virtual table is the same as with SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files and can be found in my previous blog.

 

Accessing external Delta Sharing

Also, starting from SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files has started to support external Delta Sharing.

This means you can create a remote source to Databricks Delta Sharing and also create read-only virtual tables by pointing to the tables exposed by Delta Sharing protocol.

-- create a remote source to Delta Sharing
CREATE REMOTE SOURCE <remote_source_name> ADAPTER "deltasharing" CONFIGURATION '
provider=databricks;
endpoint=<endpoint in config.share>;'
WITH CREDENTIAL TYPE 'OAUTH' USING 'access_token=<bearerToken in config.share>';

As described, a dedicated deltasharing adapter is used with the parameter provider=databricks, instead of the file adapter.

Similar to external object storage access, the remote source to Databricks Delta Sharing can also be created via the SAP HANA Database Explorer UI.

For this read-only access, Delta tables must be added to the Delta Sharing of Databricks. By creating a new recipient with a token and token lifetime, the credential (config.share) should be downloaded. Please also note that, unlike direct file access in SQL on Files, the method of creating virtual tables follows the same approach as with other SAP HANA smart data access (a.k.a., SDA) adapters.

-- create a virtual table
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE VT_TAB AT <remote_source_name>.<share_name>.<schema_name>.<table_name>;

Although Delta tables in files can be directly accessed with the file adapter, support for the Delta Sharing protocol via a dedicated deltasharing adapter can offer more flexibility if Delta Sharing is already in use and there is a requirement to use it as a unified access protocol within your organization.

Please also refer to the link below for further details.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create a Databricks Delta Sharing Remote Source

 

Two new options for CSV format

For better usability and reduced effort, two new options were introduced with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024.
One is SKIP FIRST N ROWS, which literally skips the first N number of rows when creating a virtual table by pointing to CSV files. Obviously, if the number of rows is less than N, an error will be thrown.

-- skip the first 5 rows
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE TEST_SCHEMA.TAB1 (
    A INT,
    B INT
    ) AT TEST_REMOTE."/path/to/csv/files1"
  AS CSV SKIP FIRST 5 ROW;

This is useful if there are multiple header rows, or if you intentionally ignore certain rows for any business reasons.

Another option is COLUMN LIST IN FIRST ROW, which is similar to the above option but can be used to more precisely recognize the first row as the header and ensure the match between virtual table field names and the field names in the file header.

-- recognize the first row as the header
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE TEST_SCHEMA.TAB2 (
    A INT,
    B INT
    ) AT TEST_REMOTE."/path/to/csv/files2"
  AS CSV COLUMN LIST IN FIRST ROW;

In this case, since the case sensitivity of field names is also compared, if the field name of the virtual table does not match the field name in the file header, an error is thrown.

In addition, the VIRTUAL_TABLE_FILES system view has been extended to include these two new configuration values. Please also refer to the link below for further details.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create a Virtual Table

 

Latest reader version support for Delta table

As briefly mentioned above, before QRC 01/2025, if the reader version of the Delta table was higher than 1, a virtual table could not be created. In most use cases, supporting reader version 1 was sufficient because, when converting Parquet files into Delta tables, the lowest possible reader version is generally chosen.

Nevertheless, if a specific data type like TimestampNtz (Timestamp without timezone) is included in your data or specific Delta table features like the deletion vector are enabled, a higher reader version like 3 is required, and SQL on Files could not read these before.

However, starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 01/2025, SQL on Files has begun supporting reader versions 2 and 3, meaning the existing limitation has now been fully lifted.

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, the enhanced capabilities of SAP HANA Database SQL on Files in SAP HANA Cloud provide more flexible and efficient ways to manage your data. With the ability to access vast volumes of data from well-managed object storages, you can fully leverage the robust features of the SAP HANA Cloud as a platform. This includes in-memory processing, elasticity, the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), machine learning capabilities, and more.

Furthermore, these advancements facilitate seamless data integration with SAP applications like S/4HANA, empowering you to derive even greater value by combining your object storage data with data from SAP applications. If you're interested in reading data from SAP applications like S/4HANA in SAP HANA Cloud, please refer to my blog, Taking Data Federation to the Next Level: Accessing Remote ABAP CDS View Entities in SAP HANA Cloud.

As we remain committed to innovation, stay tuned for upcoming updates that will continue to expand and enrich your SAP HANA Cloud experience.

entry.title: Re: OBPM4: '&SYSID&' Placeholder Not Replaced in Payment...
entry.content:
Read first KBA 2555646 - F110: error SG806 

  • How were the physical path and logic path defined in FILE tcode (<sysid> and not &sysid&)  FI_DME_CREATE_FILE
  • Was an event such as 21 defined  in OBPM

entry.title: Re: Steampunk is going all-in
entry.content: Good and clear explanation. easy to understand.

entry.title: Re: Does Recruitment support auto-delegation of workflows?
entry.content:
To some extent, it's possible but you need to have EC and time off implemented in the system. When there is a global assignment, termination, time off, long-term disability, or short-term disability, the requisition can be automatically transferred to another user. This setting is defined in the Recruiting Settings under Recruiting Subscriber Settings.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Error FAA_POST214 when reversing Asset Document of Le...
entry.content: Did you already perform the steps of KBA 3224015 - Error AC199 during AJAB?

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting - What is ECC t-code ABSK in S/4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content: ECC 6.0 Release 2005

entry.title: Re: Use of Content Assembly Service Destination
entry.content: This is helpful, thanks for the reply Boris.

entry.title: Re: Error 404 when trying to access a full stack CAP appl...
entry.content:
@raihan_siddiqui 

Remove the leading slash from the following part of the manifest.json.

"uri": "/odata/v4/adopter/",

Alternatively, you can add the following task in the ui5-deploy.yaml. This task removes the leading slash during the build.

  customTasks:
    - name: webide-extension-task-updateManifestJson
      afterTask: replaceVersion
      configuration:
        appFolder: webapp
        destDir: dist

entry.title: Re: Error Build Failed in CI CD
entry.content: I face the same error, did you fix it?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner – Add missing Section Tax Number to Customer Role FLCU01
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP S/4HANA, the Business Partner serves as the primary entity for managing both customer and vendor information. Within the Business Partner General Data section, you can maintain all essential cross-application master data, including addresses, communication details, tax numbers, and identification numbers.

 

Missing Information in Customer and Vendor Roles

You may have noticed that tax numbers, industry information, and identification numbers are not visible on the customer and vendor role screens (specifically roles FLCU01 and FLVN01). To view or modify tax numbers, you must switch to the Business Partner role "000000 Business Partner (Gen.)." This blog outlines simple steps to add the missing information to customer and vendor roles, focusing on tax numbers in the BP role FLCU01. You can apply these steps to other BP roles as well.

 

Screen Analysis

To gain more insights into the BDT Analyzer, refer to the blog post titled SAP S/4HANA Business Partner BDT Analyzer Usage.
To identify which screen objects are affected, you can perform a screen analysis using the transaction code BDT_ANALYZER.

At first glance, everything appears to be in order. However, there must be another reason why certain sections are not displayed. Take note of the following details regarding the missing sections:

  • Section: BUTX01 - Tax Numbers
  • Dataset: BUTX01
  • Field Group: 0114

 

Field Status Analysis

For further information on Field Status Analysis, please check the blog post titled SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Field Status Analysis.
To analyze field status, execute the transaction code CVI_CHECK_VISIBILITY and input the relevant information.

You will see the following results screen.

Now, let's review each of these check results.

 

CVI Field Mapping

If you encounter the two lines mentioned, open the specified customizing views in transaction SM30 and adjust the settings for client 000.

 

Check Dataset Field Group BP View Assignment

Open the Business Data Toolset (BDT) configuration in a new session by entering the transaction code BUPT. Navigate to the BP Views option.

Display the BP View Dataset assignment for BP View FLCU01 (which is linked to BP role FLCU01).

Here, you will notice that Dataset BUTX01 is not assigned to BP View FLCU01.

Assign Dataset BUTX01 to BP View FLCU01.

 

Check Application BP View Assignment

Tax numbers are managed by their own BDT application. Therefore, the application BUTX must be assigned to BP View FLCU01.

 

Check Changes

Field Status Analysis

To verify the changes you have made so far, run the transaction CVI_CHECK_VISIBILITY again using the data from your initial run.
From this perspective, everything appears to be correct. As you can see, the last line of the field status check has disappeared due to the customization adjustments.

 

Check BP Transaction

Finally, execute the BP transaction to confirm that tax data is now available in BP role FLCU01.

 

Conclusion

Following these steps you are able to apply missing sections to your roles in usage.

entry.title: Re: The SAP BW Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @thomaselit ,

the BW data is stored (replicated) into the Local Table (File). With the Local Table (File) you can share the Delta Table of the Local Table (File) via DeltaShare (e.g. with Databricks).

Best Regards

Christof

entry.title: Historical balances
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

can anyone here help:

Can migrated historical data (historical balance) be subsequently cancelled in the test system (SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud).
The system only generates a technical document that cannot be cancelled. No real document is generated.

Is there another way to delete migrated data?

Thank you in advance.
Best regards
N.S.

entry.title: Re: Report Editor's (se38) allowing creating only local o...

entry.title: Re: USE OF AI IN ECLIPSE
entry.content: Question was answered by patrick_winkler.

entry.title: Re: Quick & Easy Datasphere - When to use Data Flow, Transformation Flow, SQL View?
entry.content:
@yagiz_onat , Thank you for your example, I have some questions

In your transformation SQL, the returned dataset is based on transaction data, so it will return multiple materials. Since the Material master table is delta enabled, the transformation flow will use UPSERT mode to write data into target, it will run fail with error message: 'duplicate records in target'. How you tackle this problem? Thank you

entry.title: Re: EOIO in SAP CI Using JMS - Exclusive Mode
entry.content: @vinaymittal Are you sure JMSRetries header available for Exclusive queues? I have not seen this so far. I can only see it for Non-Exclusive queues.

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议
entry.content:
@Fang 

Hi Fang, could you try to run IMG in the Open search box and check the configuration activity for the Define Withholding Tax keys in the following path?

Accounting > Tax Management > Withholding Tax > Withholding Tax Calculation > Define Withholding Tax keys

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP公有云台湾本地化-代扣税实施建议
SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model – Introducing Team Collection
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP Community readers!

As part of continuous enhancements in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM) to be able to provide a seamless experience and flexibility to its users, let me give you the latest feature enhancement in the Teams application.

Currently, users can only assign one team as a Performer or Reviewer in process activities in Process Management which is somehow limited in case multiple teams are needed for process reviews. Apart from this, users still have to assign multiple teams across SAP PaPM Cloud SM applications such as Environments Access Management and in Reader/Writer team for analytic privileges. Even though this remains functional, users are seeking extra flexibility in teams management.

With the new implementation, users can now create a team collection that can contain  . This new feature is especially helpful when users are already part of a team but also need to be included in other teams for specific applications.

This also means that all the child teams will dynamically adapt the access or authorizations of their parent team. Furthermore, it gives the users the flexibility to assign multiple teams as a team collection to Performer/Reviewer in Process Management or Reader/Writer for analytic privileges instead of assigning multiple single teams. 

To show you this amazing new feature enhancement, let's start with the new UI in Teams application. Upon accessing the Teams screen, you will see the new column “Child Teams” in Teams panel. In the Child Teams column, it will simply display all the child teams of the parent team.

 

Creation of Team Collection

The creation of a team collection can be done by adding a single or multiple child teams to a Team.

To add a child team:
  1. In the Teams application, select an existing team from the Teams list.
  2. Choose the Edit button.
  3. Under Child Teams section, click on the dropdown.
  4. Choose one or more teams from the list.
  5. Choose Confirm.
    Spoiler
    The child team added in a team will be automatically visible under the Child Teams column in the form of a hyperlink. Once clicked, it will allow the user to focus or display the specific child team.

After successful creation of a team collection, it is ready to be used in applications such as Environments Access as Authorized Teams, Reader/Writer teams for analytic privileges, and Performer/Reviewer teams in process activities.

The user can also add a new team collection as follows:
  1. In the Teams application, choose the Add button from the Teams panel.
  2. Define Name and Description of the team.
  3. Under Child Teams section, click on the dropdown.
  4. Choose team/s from the list.
  5. Choose Confirm.
  6. Optionally, the user can add users to the created team collection by adding users in Users panel.

The user can remove/unlink the child team from a specific team collection via the Edit Team button and simply remove the child team from the list. In any case, if all child teams of the team collection were removed, the team collection can still exist as a regular team.

To remove/unlink child teams:
  1. In the Teams application, select an existing team collection.
  2. Choose the Edit button from the Teams panel.
  3. Under Child Teams section, remove the child teams.
  4. Choose Confirm.Note: The removed/unlinked child teams will no longer have the access or authorization of the parent team and will just exist independently.
Adding Team Collection as child team:

In addition, a team collection can also be a child of another team or multiple teams, which makes it more versatile as shown below.

Note: Circular Parent-Child relationship is not possible wherein a parent team cannot be assigned to its child team (ex. Team X1 is a child of Team X and Team X is a child of Team X1).

 

Assigning and Managing Team Collection in Applications

Prerequisites: Create team collections.

For example:

Team CollectionChild Teams
TEAM ATEAM1
TEAM2
TEAM BTEAM3
Environments Access Screen

The created team collection can now be used as Authorized Teams in private environments.

  1. In the Environments Access screen, choose an environment.
  2. Choose the Edit Access for Environment(s) button from the header.
  3. Choose Private, then assign TEAM A as Authorized Teams.
  4. Choose Confirm.

In this scenario, parent TEAM A with child teams TEAM1 and TEAM2 will have access to the private environment, while TEAM B will not be able to see the private environment.

Note: Users should not have ENVIRONMENTS_ACCESS_ALL role.

The parent TEAM B with child team TEAM3 will not have access to the private environment.

Reader and Writer Teams in Master Data with Analytic Privileges

The created team collection can now be used as Reader and Writer in master data with analytic privileges.

  1. In the Modeling screen, choose a Characteristic field with master data.
  2. Assign the team collection as Reader and Writer in master data values as per below.
  3. Choose Save. 
  4. Access the Input/Output activity (Editable Query).

In this scenario, TEAM A will be able to view and update on the data rows with CUST1, CUST2 and CUST3.
While TEAM B will be able to view and update on the data rows with CUST4 and CUST5. Users that are not part of the Reader and Writer is expected to not see any data rows.

For more info, you may visit related blog post SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud: Analytic Privileges

Process Management

The users can have the capability to assign multiple teams in a team collection and assign as Performer and Reviewer in process activities. The teams that are part of the performer and reviewer will also receive the email notifications for every activity action.

  1. In the Process Management screen, choose or create a process instance.
  2. Assign team collections below in all process activities.
    Performer: TEAM A
    Reviewer:  TEAM B
    For example: 
  3. Choose Save.

    Note: The users that are part of the team should enable Email Notifications via User Preferences to be able to receive emails.

In this scenario, users part of TEAM A (Performer) can perform Submit activity and users that are part of TEAM B (Reviewer) can perform Reject/Approve the activities.

  1. User part of TEAM A (child team: TEAM1) can perform submit and receive email notification.
  2. User part of TEAM A (child team: TEAM2) can perform submit and receive email notification.
  3. User part of TEAM B (child team: TEAM3) can perform approval and receive email notification.

If you want to learn more about dual control mechanism in SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model, you may visit this related blog post SAP PaPM Cloud Dual Control Mechanism & Process Instance Filtering

For more information, you may check our Standard Model Application Help: Teams 

That’s it! I hope you find this new feature enhancement useful and your work with SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model better and stay tuned for more related blog posts, you may also check the same feature in SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model via SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: What’s New as of 2024-12-12?

See you on the next one!

entry.title: Stock Availability Access
entry.content:
Hello,

We essentially need to grant access at three levels:

  1. Procurement Department – Needs access to stock to check current availability, procurement-relevant quantities, In Process (PO qty not receive) to manage replenishment. They should have access to item cost but ideally to head of department not all procurement officers.
  2. Sales Team – to check sellable stock when creating orders. They need to view sellable stock, excluding restricted or blocked stock, Booked by other sales orders or quote and in staging being prepared. Also ideally for manager available-to-promise (ATP) stock

ATP stock = Unrestricted stock + Receipts (ex: confirmed POs, production orders) – Requirements (ex: sales orders, reservations)

  1. Warehouse Team – to monitor physical and staged inventory

At present, we’ve been provided access, to two apps:

  • Stock – Multiple Materials ( then we were told not to use it)
  • Display Warehouse Stock

Unfortunately, both apps expose cost-related data, which is not appropriate for all users. I’ve already requested immediate removal of cost visibility and revocation of access to prevent internal exposure of sensitive data.

The quantity displayed is not the Sellable stock which is the mostly needed.

Some warehouse users (though not all) have access to the EWM Warehouse Monitor.

From my review, this monitor provides the most accurate stock representation. However, it includes extensive functions that go beyond basic review needs and does not appear to support a view-only access mode, additionally doesn't include the available stock at IM locations.

I would value your recommendation on:

  • Which apps we should use for each department
  • How we can restrict views to only necessary data (especially excluding cost)
  • Whether there is a lighter or read-only version of the EWM Monitor or a better-suited alternative for general warehouse staff

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Any one from SAP Build Team could assist please???

entry.title: Re: IDOC Extension
entry.content: Answered by Sandra_Rossi.

entry.title: Connecting a remote SQL Anywhereto an system SAP using DBCONN and ODBC
entry.content:
Hello,

we Have a remote SAP SQL Anywhere that we need to connect to our sap system, so, we can access the SQL Anywhere Tables from SAP-ABAP. I can't find any good tutorials for it

can anyone help on this? 

regards,
Nabil

entry.title: Re: Introduction to SAP DRC and Integration aspects
entry.content:
Hi Nitin, 

I have a question in regards to AIF being compulsary part of the solution. We have a Private S/4 System and using the DRC_BTP Cloud edition. Is there any alternative to AIF for Exchanging data between S4 and BTP? 

second question is how much will AIF cost us, is it a fixed cost (License Cost) or variable Cost (per document/message)? 

would be glad to hear some feedback. 

regards, 

Rashed Popalzai

entry.title: Consume material via production process doesn't trigger UI's refresh in order POD
entry.content:
Hello,

I have enabled notifications in an Order POD in SAP DMC. As expected, whenever I start or complete a phase from a production process everything updates correctly.

But whenever I enter the "Material consumption" tab if I consume some material via production process the UI is not updated (I need to refresh the page)

The production process is the following one:

The production process works as expected. Whenever it is triggered, the material is consumed correctly (in fact, if I refresh the UI I can see the material's quantity updated)

 

 

I have already enabled all notifications in the POD designer

Is there a way to implement a custom event handler in the plugin extension project? Or is there a way to trigger the UI's update from a production process? Or is there another way to accomplish this task?
Thank you

entry.title: Posting rejected (UPDATE_REJECT).Order 104XXXX could not be saved and Message No.COIS008
Re: Asset Accounting - What is ECC t-code ABSK in S/4Hana...
entry.content:
On my oldest ECC systems (but not 20 years old) these transaction types were related to

  • 200 - Retirement without revenue
  • 210 - Retirement with revenue

So SAP transactions such as 

  • F-92 - Retirement With Customer
  • ABAO Asset Sale Without Customer
  • ABAV Asset Retirement by Scrapping

Would relate to S/4HANA Cloud App id such as F7107 - Post Asset Retirement (Feel free to look for any Post Asset App id from the link.

But I did not found any ABSK transaction (sorry)

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps

entry.title: SAP BUILD importing .mtar file error
entry.content:
Hello,

We changed our plan from free to standard and we are importing our build apps projects. I am experiencing this error for 3 out of 6 projects that we have. I double checked and the project is not existing. I also tried creating a new project with the same name and I was able to create it and delete it.

I found one solution in google, but I don't have access to this link.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-knowledge-base/workaround-for-error-importing-projects-in-sap-build-apps/ta-p/220085

Can anyone know what can we do to upload our projects? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS issue with using replace : Number of positions > maximum number (1333) for data type
entry.content:
As stated above, the command works as described. But that's not how we use the command: especially in the typical usage to replace one occurences.

But, there's one easy way to solve the problem: 

define view entity zzlk_vb_message 
as select from t100
{
  replace(
    replace(
      t100.text,
      cast( '&1' as abap.sstring(50)),
        'Something_to_insert'
      ),
      cast( '&2' as abap.sstring(50) ),
      'Something_to_insert'
  ) as message 
}

 With this simple cast the syntax check allows this combination and the result is as expected.

entry.title: Issue creating storage bins via LS01N – Missing LGTYP and LGBER values
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently facing an issue while trying to create new storage bins using transaction LS01N.

The problem is that the fields Storage Type (LGTYP) and Storage Section (LGBER) are missing or not maintained – they show up empty, and I can’t proceed.

I assume these values are supposed to be predefined in the system, but it looks like they’re not.

 

My questions are:

1. Where and how can I maintain or create Storage Types (LGTYP) and Storage Sections (LGBER)?

2. What are the relevant transactions or SPRO paths to configure this?

3. Are there any important dependencies or things to watch out for when setting them up?

Would really appreciate a step-by-step guide or any advice from someone who's done this before. 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Historical balances
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, once you migrate historical financial data (such as opening balances or historical balances) using the "Migrate Your Data" app, these are stored as technical documents, and they cannot be cancelled or reversed in the traditional sense (i.e., no FI document is posted that can be reversed with FB08 or similar).

This is standard system behavior, and you're correct in observing that « the system only generates a technical document that cannot be cancelled ».

 

Here’s some options you can study on :

Option 1: No Deletion Tool Available in Public Cloud

SAP does not provide a standard tool in S/4HANA Public Cloud to delete migrated data. This restriction ensures data consistency and aligns with audit-compliant practices.

 

Option 2: Reset the Migration in Test System via SAP Support

If you're in a test client or test system, and you need to reset migrated data, the standard approach is:

Create an SAP incident and request SAP to reset the migration content for the relevant object (e.g., historical balances, open items, etc.).

This is the only supported method for clean-up, especially if the technical objects are already created and cannot be deleted manually.

What you’ll need to provide:

  • System ID / client
  • Migration object used (e.g., "FI – Historical G/L Balances")
  • Confirmation that it is not a production system
  • Reason for reset (e.g., testing error, incorrect balances, testing re-migration)

 

Option 3: Overwrite Balances with New Migration

If the reset is not possible or feasible, you can consider re-uploading the balances with reversed or corrected values via a new migration object run.

Example:

  • Original upload: +100 USD to GL account
  • New upload: -100 USD to same GL account → Net effect = 0

However, this approach:

  • Creates additional technical entries (it doesn't delete or overwrite the old one),
  • May not be suitable for full clean-up in testing scenarios.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How can I change growing to scroll in a Fiori element...
entry.content:
ok, possible to include a custom section in Object Page Noel?

And inside that a smart table (scrollable by default)

  <SmartTable id="mySmartTable"
              entitySet="ProductSet"
              tableType="Table"
              useVariantManagement="true"
              useTablePersonalisation="true"
              useExportToExcel="true"
              header="Products"
              smartFilterId="SmartFilterBar"
              demandPopin="false"
              enableAutoBinding="true">
  </SmartTable>

entry.title: In the Product Description Page, the title attribute on the cx-media is being over ridden by alt
entry.content:
I have upgraded to Spartacus 2211.32 and noticed that the "title" tag on cx-media no longer works. I have created a custom "productImageComponent" by extended the existing "ProductImageZoomProductImagesComponent" and using the "title" attribute on the cx-media. But the title is not visible when I hover on the image. Seems like its being over ridden by the "alt" attribute.

Seems like OOTB, alt and title are set to same value. Is there a way to over ride this?

<img
      [loading]="loading"
      [alt]="media.alt"
      [title]="media.alt"
      [src]="media.src"
      [attr.role]="media.role"
      (load)="loadHandler()"
      (error)="errorHandler()"
    />

entry.title: EWM Physical Inventory Error : Enter a Warehouse Number Message no. /SCWM/LC1194
entry.content: Hi gurus, I am getting an error while performing a physical inventory Please help me out of this.

entry.title: Agentic Testing and Simulation with Int4 Suite's SAP Business Knowledge Graph
entry.content:
Intro 

Have you ever wondered why simulation and test automation software is not accessible in a easy way to everyone for SAP projects? Now with Agentic Testing and Simulation based on Int4 Suite's SAP Business Knowledge Graph it's no longer the case with Int4 Suite. You can use your enterprise AI and to simulate any 3rd party test system and run any automated SAP Business process test defined in Int4 Suite.

Why use Agents and AI for Testing and Simulation of SAP applications?

The Int4 Suite stores millions of historical electronic messages (test cases) pulled from various integration platforms and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC backends using Int4 Suite Robotic Crawler, as shown in the figure below.

These messages can be grouped into folders and executed across development and test environments. However, this traditional approach comes with two major challenges:

a) Finding the Right Test Cases - Even with our powerful Test Case Analyzer, searching through millions of test messages can be overwhelming. Imagine needing to find all messages related to incoming sales orders for:

Customer: 5002334

Materials: T-DK0001 and B-234234

Even with perfectly structured folders, locating the right data might still take several minutes. Multiply that effort across teams and use cases, it adds up.

b) Accessibility and Democratization of Testing - At Int4, we champion citizen testing. Testing and simulation shouldn’t be limited to users with automation expertise. Anyone should be able to run tests, manual or automated, easily and intuitively. That’s why Int4 Suite isn’t licensed by the number of users. We believe in unrestricted access for all who need to test SAP-integrated business processes.

How Agentic Testing and Simulation Solves These Challenges?

With Agentic Testing and Simulation built on Int4 Suite’s SAP Business Knowledge Graph, we can either eliminate or significantly reduce the two main pain points above:

a) Smarter Test Case Discovery - by combining:

  • Historical messages,

  • Master data,

  • And entity relationships,

our intelligent agents enable users to perform semantic and business-context-based searches across all test cases. You can now search using natural language or business-specific criteria and execute relevant tests instantly similarly as shown in Figure below. 

b) AI-Driven Chat for Testing - thanks to enterprise AI and chat-based interfaces, anyone, from any SAP S/4HANA or SAP BTP project, can now access and use testing features without needing to understand the underlying software mechanics.

This brings simulation and automated testing closer to business analysts, project managers, and functional consultants, not just developers or testers.

Why Int4 Suite’s SAP Business Knowledge Graph is a Game Changer?

Having access to millions of test messages is powerful. But being able to query those test cases from a business perspective makes it revolutionary.

Instead of filtering only by material numbers or quantities, imagine asking:

  • Pharmaceutical industry:
    "Show me test cases of sales orders containing materials flagged as high-risk."

  • FMCG industry:
    "Find sales orders from partners where deliveries were most delayed in the last 3–6 months—especially when it led to lost priority unloading slots."

To enable these kinds of queries, the Business Knowledge Graph links test data with master data and maps relationships between them—unlocking far more intuitive, business-relevant insights.

Where to Learn More About Int4 Suite?

👉Visit our SAP Learning Course - Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization

entry.title: WFA on Hana Succession Metric: Applicable Succession Positions Count
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 :

We have a query regarding the WFA Succession measure Applicable Succession Positions. The client is using a position-based nomination method for Succession.

According to the specification, the Applicable Succession Positions measure should count the number of positions at the end of the reporting period. However, it appears to be counting the number of unique Successor IDs instead.

We noticed this during our review of the WFA Drill to Detail. The data provided focuses solely on Successor details, rather than on active positions. For example, if a department has 100 active positions but only 20 successors (with unique position IDs), the measure returns a value of 20.

Given the limited scope of the current measure, we wanted to understand whether the measure’s definition could be clearer or whether the code itself needs to be adjusted to better support position-based nominations.

How would we tweak this code to reflect the expected count—i.e. the number of positions rather than count of successors’ positions?

Thank you.

Best regards,

Zaklin Perosa

@mikehoekstra1

@timokapanen 

Please help.

entry.title: Re: Firebase integration not working with SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
My conclusion is the component problem: "LogicEmail & password authentication (Firebase Auth)" . I create a new /clean test project just the 1 screen: login - default components - just change the connections / databases.. all the same response: "the user / password is incorrect. " . 2 days ago, all databases connected ... now.. none.
aditional information - I test one old version (3 weeks ago) published in firebase.. and this version work the login.. but.. I compiled with the last version.. and now, dont work anymore.

So. for me.. we have one problem in this actual version.

entry.title: Interactive Fragmented/Adobe Forms using Adobe Live Cycle
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Developed fragmented adobe forms using Adobe Live Cycle designer which is linked to RAP model at the backend, currently stuck with one requirement to make the fragmented form interactive as business wants checkboxes to be interactive and should be able to alter checkbox data and save in db the changes from front end. We are currently displaying the adobe form using UI5 front end and using pdf container just to display form.

Can anyone help me making the adobe form/fragmented forms interactive. Any suggestion guiding on the same is really appreciated.

Thanks & Best Regards!!

entry.title: Re: Rounding Logic Documentation: Rounding from 0.5 in SAP ABAP
Event Based Product Costing & Actual Overhead Application
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I do have one question.

In Event Based Product Costing, Actual Overheads are getting charged immediately on to Production Order if we have maintained Actual OH Rate / Percentage in Costing Sheet OH Rates.

Where as in Actual Business Condition, Determination of Actual OH Rate is only possible after Period End Closing.  How to handle this?

How to allocate actual incurred overheads on to product during Actual Costing??

I have gone through Actual Costing 33Q, Event Based Product Costing (3F0) Processes, but I could not find the answer.  

Please guide me in this scenario.

Thanks in Advance

Nitin

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps

BR, Timo Kapanen

entry.title: Out-of-the-Box Document Grounding in SAP AI Core: RAG with SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Introduction

As enterprises adopt large language models (LLMs), grounding responses in trusted sources is essential to ensure accuracy and contextual relevance. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses this need by dynamically retrieving relevant content to guide model outputs.
SAP AI Core simplifies this process through its document grounding capability, which allows developers to enhance LLMs with enterprise-specific knowledge. Most notably, help.sap.com is already grounded by default—meaning you can immediately retrieve content from SAP’s official product documentation without any setup. In addition, SAP AI Core supports integration with custom data repositories such as SharePoint, AWS S3, and Metadata, providing flexibility for broader use cases.

This blog focuses on testing retrieval from help.sap.com, showcasing how easy it is to build a RAG workflow using out-of-the-box SAP content. Let’s dive in.

Prerequisite

  • Subscription to SAP AI Core (Extended plan)

Restrictions (as of this blog publication):

  • SAP AI Core free tier is only available in SAP BTP free tier, not SAP BTP trial.
  • The Extended Plan for SAP AI Core is only available with CPEA or BTPEA entitlement. You can find more details about pricing and service plans in the Discovery Center here.
  • Not all SAP product documentation is indexed yet within the Help Portal data repository. However, most major SAP solutions are already covered. If you notice occasional gaps or less relevant retrievals, keep in mind that the index is continuously improving over time.

Initial Setup

Step 1: Complete foundational setup of SAP AI Core instance

You can refer to this help documentation that already provide step by step guides here (Enabling the service in Cloud Foundry). Ensure that you choose the extended plan in order to use generative AI capabilities.

Step 2: Download and import Postman collection

To interact with SAP AI Core programmatically—whether for workflow orchestration, document grounding setup, or managing resources—developers can leverage the prebuilt Postman collection available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

You can refer to this help documentation (Using a 3rd Party API Platform) on the procedure to download JSON collection from SAP Business Accelerator Hub, and import into Postman. In this blog, you will need to download & import these OpenAPI JSON collections:

  • SAP AI Core - AI Core
  • SAP AI Core - Grounding
  • SAP AI Core - Orchestration


Alternatively, you’re free to use your preferred API platform tool—such as Bruno, cURL, or others—based on your workflow and tooling preferences. In this blog, I will be using Postman for demonstration purposes.

Quick Start

REST APIs that we need for this blog are as follows:

  • /SAP AI Core /AI Core /Resource Group > POST /admin/resourceGroups
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > GET /retrieval/dataRepositories
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > POST /retrieval/search
  • /SAP AI Core /Orchestration /Completion > POST /completion
Step 1: Create a resource group for document grounding

Before we begin, we a resource group that segment our document grounding workloads. Use the following API endpoint to create a resource group: POST /admin/resourceGroups. Include the following JSON payload in the request body. Make sure to add the label key "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding" with the value "true"—this is required to enable document grounding capabilities within the resource group

{
  "resourceGroupId": "<input resource group id here>",
  "labels": [
    {
      "key": "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding",
      "value": "true"
    }
  ]
}

In my example, I'm using 'grounding' as my resource group id, but it can be any unique name / id of your choice.

Step 2: List all data repository objects (Optional)

In case you're interested to see all data repository objects that are available in your AI Core resource group, you can use this API: GET /retrieval/dataRepositories

As shown in the response, a default repository named 'help.sap.com' is already provisioned. This built-in integration allows you to perform RAG directly on official SAP Help documentation—enabling immediate access to curated enterprise knowledge without additional setup.

To perform get all data repository objects, remember to update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request headers. You should see the output below; for my case, I have an additional data repository object from SharePoint, thus another entry showing 'vector' in addition to 'help.sap.com'.



Step 3: Retrieval search via Vector (Approach #1)

There are 2 ways to query grounded documents from data repositories be it from data repository type 'vector' or type 'help.sap.com'. One of the way is using vector retrieval search. This API queries the connected data repository and returns the most relevant content chunks based on the user-provided query: POST /retrieval/search.

To perform retrieval search on 'help.sap.com' data repository, same as above update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, and use this JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "query": "",
  "filters": [
    {
      "id": "filter1",
      "dataRepositoryType": "help.sap.com",
      "dataRepositories": [
        "*"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

In my example, I want to know the official migration guide for SAP PI/PO, thus entering this question "show me migration guide for SAP Process Orchestration" in "query".

Retrieval search results as follows:



Step 4: Retrieval search via Orchestration (Approach #2)

This is another way to query grounded documents via orchestration service. What it does it integrates document grounding configurations and filters to provide precise results. With the orchestration service, you can interact with LLMs from multiple providers through a unified interface, without changing your application code. Switching models is as simple as updating a configuration parameter, making it easy to compare performance or adapt to different requirements. It also supports advanced features like prompt templating, content filtering, data masking, and grounding for secure and context-aware AI integration.

To get started, you’ll need to create at least one orchestration deployment—or use the default deployment within your resource group. If no deployments are found, please refer to this tutorial for guidance tutorial.

To perform retrieval search using orchestraction service with 'help.sap.com' as data repository source, make sure that you update the "deploymentId" URL path variable and update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, then use this example JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "orchestration_config": {
    "module_configurations": {
      "grounding_module_config": {
        "type": "document_grounding_service",
        "config": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "id": "filter1",
              "data_repositories": [
                "*"
              ],
              "search_config": {
                "max_chunk_count": 5
              },
              "data_repository_type": "help.sap.com"
            }
          ],
          "input_params": [
            "groundingRequest"
          ],
          "output_param": "groundingOutput"
        }
      },
      "llm_module_config": {
        "model_name": "",
        "model_params": {},
        "model_version": ""
      },
      "templating_module_config": {
        "template": [
          {
            "role": "user",
            "content": ""
          }
        ],
        "defaults": {}
      },
      "filtering_module_config": {
        "input": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "output": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "input_params": {
    "groundingRequest": ""
  }
}

Orchestration service result as follows:

Essentially what I need is the 'message' > 'content' from 'orchestration_result'. If you want to see the complete orchestration schema, refer to this link.

Conclusion

As shown above, the retrieval search returns highly relevant results for the given query—including document chunks closely aligned with the intended topic. This significantly reduces the time spent manually searching through documentation, making the process of grounding large language models both efficient and precise. As SAP continues to integrate AI into its business technology ecosystem, capabilities like these will play a key role in delivering intelligent, reliable enterprise applications.

For more information on SAP AI Core

  • Exchange knowledge: SAP Community
  • Learn more: Help Portal | Roadmap | Orchestration
  • Explore: Product Information

Special thanks to product experts that provide me advise during my testing: @Sumedha_Garg @gesine_stanienda . 
Also do check out other blog from my colleague @rachel_gan  on "Setting up SAP Document Grounding for Chat Apps" with SharePoint as data repository source.

entry.content:

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

 

Have you checked the SAP note below ?

2539550 - More gain/loss lines generated with amount 0.00 in LC1 or LC2 when clearing

 

It might be useful to answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Delete Business Partner in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is not possible
entry.content:
Hi,

Support should be able to help you.

you can also utilize the CIC 

  1. For guidance on how to contact the SAP Customer Interaction Center, please refer to SAP Note 560499– Customer Interaction Center: Hotline - Email - Chat
  2. CIC Hotline à 1 800 677 7271, Option 2
  3. Expert Chat à https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#incident/create (For quick resolutions on new issues!)
  4. Schedule an Expert à https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/sae (For convenient, personalized SAP support on your schedule!)
  5. Customer Portal à https://support.sap.com

There could be some transactions or other data associated with the BP preventing the deletion.

entry.title: Re: EOIO in SAP CI Using JMS - Exclusive Mode
entry.content:
@devang_mandli  Yes I assumed it would be there but then I checked its not there. The only option is to maintain a counter in a datastore with datastore id as "1" that holds the message id of the JMS message and updates a counter if its already there in data store, if its not there then overwrites the existing entry with a counter 0 and with the new message id. 

But honestly this is just like how the inventor of the first car forgot to brakes in it.

entry.title: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relevant to cost accounting Message no. KI235
entry.content:
We are running an asset depreciation job in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and receiving the following error:

Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relevant to cost accounting
Message no. KI235

The asset in question already has a cost center assigned in the asset master data, and we do not want to override or use any other cost center.

Could you please help clarify why the system is still requiring a separate CO assignment during depreciation, even though it’s already maintained in the asset master?
Also, is there a way to ensure the system always pulls the cost center from the asset master automatically during depreciation?

We want to avoid maintaining default account assignments or substitutions for this scenario, since the correct cost center is already maintained on the asset.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAP TM Complete Configuration Guide
entry.content: Hi, Can anyone share the SAP TM complete configuration guide to my mail id - cska29031991@gmail.com

entry.title: Unable to find the scope item IDs for SAP PS for SAP cloud private 2023-FSP02
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are using SAP Cloud Private Edition 2023-FSP02 for our customer. I need the SAP PS Customer Project and Capital Project scope item IDs to link in cloud ALM. I downloaded the master BPML from SAP portal and it has PS10 and PS11 respectively for both the project types but there's no documentation maintained on the SAP portal under these scope IDs.

Can I use 1NT-Project Control Finance and BNA-Period end closing for these project types? Please suggest the solution as I need to finalize the scope item IDs as soon as possible.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Unable to Create/Post STO Purchase order Using BO/API
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We're trying to post a STO Purchase Order using Standard API & same we try with business object, But we get a error of (MessageClass='APPL_MM_PUR_PO',MessageNumber='065'  Short Text = Use a supported purchase order item category for item &. See long text.) 

please help with this to find out the way to resolve this.

 

Link - https://my418769-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurchaseOrder

Payload - 
{
    "CompanyCode": "1000",
    "PurchaseOrderType": "UD",
    "Supplier": "1004",
    "PurchasingOrganization": "1000",
    "PurchasingGroup": "003",
    "_PurchaseOrderItem": [
        {
            "Plant": "1007",
            "OrderQuantity": 7,
            "NetPriceAmount": 0,
            "NetPriceQuantity": 1,
            "PurchaseOrderItemCategory": "7",
            "TaxCode": "2E",
            "DocumentCurrency" : "",
            "OrderPriceUnit" : "",
            "PurchaseOrderQuantityUnit" : " "
           
        }
    ]
}

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Thank you @timokapanen help will be much appreciated.

entry.title: BW Query statistics not available for CDS-based Queries on transient providers in S/4 HANA system
entry.content:
Hi,

I have built BW Queries on top of CDS Views exposed as transient providers in the S/4HANA system. However, I am unable to find the statistics for these queries.

I have enabled statistics by setting it to 'ON' in Transaction RSDDSTAT, but the data is still not being captured in the underlying table RSDDSTAT_OLAP.

Could you please help me with how to capture statistics for BW Queries in S/4HANA?

Thanks,

K Srinivas

entry.title: Purchase Order forms
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

 

Is it possible to add condition record lines in purchase order form in line items details 

eg. If we purchase metals like silver copper prices  in condition record can we make display of it in po print for supplier to know how many meters and what is the market price as of ordering and then net amount to be dispalyed and leads highly appreciated .

expected these prices to be displayed in Purchase order .

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-Ons Samples Preview
entry.content:
Hi, thank you for the useful information - it was very helpful! I tried using the Datepicker as a custom widget SAC, and while the widget itself works, the Builder Panel doesn’t show any options to link it to a data model. As a result, the Datepicker doesn’t actually filter any data. What steps do I need to take to make it work correctly with the data model?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Outbound Process – From DC site to Customer in S/4 HANA embedded EWM TM – Advanced Shipping & Re
entry.content:
Hi Prashant,

Thanks for this blog, it was helpful. 

This process works for us in normal outbound scenario. However, when we enable the compliance check in GTS system (SAP-GTS in a separate box), we have an issue in processing the request in EWM. We are unable to create warehouse task with a message shown as below. If you could help us in where to look for resolution of this issue.

Thanks

Shivang

entry.title: Requirement of an app for excel upload functionality using BTP BAS, please guide me
CUSTOM LOGIC FOR INCOTERMS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

we have a custom logic for a company code already that triggers incoterms in PO line items .

However, we wanted to have same functionality for another company code to trigger in PO line item level.

Can we extend same custom logic for another company code or we need to create new logic for new company 

if we can extend, how can we achieve it .

entry.title: Unable to change Microsoft Teams SF standard logo
entry.content:
Hi All,

Is there a way to edit or replace the default SuccessFactors logo in Microsoft Teams with our company logo? I'd appreciate any insights if anyone has tried or implemented this customization.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: EWM Physical Inventory Error : Enter a Warehouse Numb...
entry.content: Olá, conseguir resolver implementando a nota 3033426 - '/SCWM/LC1 194 Entrar um número de depósito' ao contar o produto avaliado em partição

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

The message you get is due to a missing entry for the G/L account 64004000 in the default account assignment configuration that you can find in the SSCUI 102639.

CBC path :

I hope this will help you solve your problem.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Account 64004000 requires an account assignment relev...
entry.content: Hello Jeremy! Yes I understand that the Manage Default Account Assignment could be the solution, but that is not what we want. Why do we have to use a default cost center when there's already one maintained in the fixed asset master data?

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE HADR - database replication limit
entry.content: Thank you Chris, all is clear now.

entry.title: Re: Stock Availability Access
entry.content:
Hi @AliSaad,

fro ATP stock, you should use the application Monitor Product Availability which is equivalent of CO09 transaction from SAP GUI. This app should meet the requirements which you mentioned for the Sales Team.

Unfortunately for the EWM-related questions, I am not able to advice.  

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Migrating BP Counterparty role (TR0151)
entry.content:
Hello Nilesh,

 

Thanks for excellent blog. 
Can you also update your test Excel/txt file for upload?

Regards,

Faruk
